prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(enroll_member assault_element_alpha)
(assign_role_to_member assault_element_alpha role_token_a)
(assign_tactic_to_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(confirm_member_configuration assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_utility_to_element_route assault_element_alpha entry_route_alpha utility_smoke)
(remove_tactic_from_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(enroll_member support_element_alpha)
(assign_role_to_member support_element_alpha role_token_b)
(assign_tactic_to_member support_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(confirm_member_configuration support_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(reserve_sector_for_support support_element_alpha cover_sector_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_gear_to_member support_element_alpha gear_breacher_kit)
(confirm_support_sector_with_gear support_element_alpha cover_sector_alpha gear_breacher_kit)
(assemble_subteam_with_route_sync assault_element_alpha support_element_alpha entry_route_alpha cover_sector_alpha subteam_alpha)
(remove_tactic_from_member support_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_tactic_to_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(finalize_element_route_with_utility assault_element_alpha entry_route_alpha tactic_entry utility_smoke)
(remove_tactic_from_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_tactic_to_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(provision_subteam subteam_alpha assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(remove_tactic_from_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(remove_gear_from_member support_element_alpha gear_breacher_kit)
(enroll_member coordinator_alpha)
(assign_role_to_member coordinator_alpha role_token_c)
(assign_tactic_to_member coordinator_alpha tactic_entry)
(confirm_member_configuration coordinator_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_gear_to_member coordinator_alpha gear_breacher_kit)
(coordinator_attach_extraction_marker coordinator_alpha extraction_marker_1)
(coordinator_attach_target_tag coordinator_alpha target_tag_alpha tactic_entry)
(coordinator_assign_target_gear coordinator_alpha gear_breacher_kit)
(coordinator_attach_extraction_for_target coordinator_alpha extraction_marker_1)
(coordinator_confirm_target_and_authorize coordinator_alpha)
(assign_initiator_to_member coordinator_alpha signal_marker_primary tactic_entry)
(propagate_initiator_to_coordinator coordinator_alpha role_token_c signal_marker_primary)
(remove_tactic_from_member coordinator_alpha tactic_entry)
(remove_gear_from_member coordinator_alpha gear_breacher_kit)
(coordinator_detach_extraction_marker coordinator_alpha extraction_marker_1)
(enroll_member coordinator_bravo)
(assign_role_to_member coordinator_bravo role_token_c)
(assign_tactic_to_member coordinator_bravo tactic_entry)
(confirm_member_configuration coordinator_bravo tactic_entry)
(coordinator_claim_secondary_objective coordinator_bravo secondary_objective_b)
(attach_objective_marker_to_subteam coordinator_bravo objective_marker_b subteam_alpha)
(coordinator_configure_loadout coordinator_bravo objective_marker_b subteam_alpha tactic_entry secondary_objective_b)
(coordinator_attach_breach_charge coordinator_bravo breach_charge_1)
(assign_gear_to_member coordinator_bravo gear_breacher_kit)
(coordinator_prepare_with_breach coordinator_bravo breach_charge_1 gear_breacher_kit objective_marker_b subteam_alpha)
(coordinator_attach_extraction_marker coordinator_bravo extraction_marker_1)
(coordinator_activate_extraction_with_route_flag coordinator_bravo extraction_marker_1 objective_marker_b subteam_alpha)
(coordinator_attach_sync_marker coordinator_bravo sync_marker_a)
(coordinator_open_execution_window coordinator_bravo assault_element_alpha support_element_alpha tactic_entry sync_marker_a)
(coordinator_finalize_and_authorize coordinator_bravo)
(assign_initiator_to_member coordinator_bravo signal_marker_primary tactic_entry)
(propagate_initiator_to_coordinator coordinator_bravo role_token_c signal_marker_primary)
(remove_tactic_from_member coordinator_bravo tactic_entry)
(commit_assault_element assault_element_alpha subteam_alpha)
(commit_support_element support_element_alpha subteam_alpha)
(assign_tactic_to_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_initiator_to_member assault_element_alpha signal_marker_primary tactic_entry)
(propagate_initiator_to_assault_element assault_element_alpha role_token_a signal_marker_primary)
(remove_tactic_from_member assault_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_tactic_to_member support_element_alpha tactic_entry)
(assign_initiator_to_member support_element_alpha signal_marker_primary tactic_entry)
(propagate_initiator_to_support_element support_element_alpha role_token_b signal_marker_primary)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for "stealth_assault_team_partitioning". I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task involves solving a planning problem in PDDL, specifically "stealth_assault_team_partitioning."  
- The goal is to generate an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions.  

## Formatting the Output  
- Each action must be output on a new line in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).  
- The output should only include the ground PDDL actions, with no additional details.

## Examining Resources and Roles  
- The problem involves two coordinators, alpha and bravo, managing assault and support elements like assault_element_alpha and support_element_alpha, with shared resources like one tactic_entry and gear_breacher_kit.  
- Limited resources, such as one signal_marker_primary and utility_smoke, suggest sequential actions, possibly restricting simultaneous initiations.  

## Analyzing Authorization Process  
- To commit members, elements need to be authorized for execution via actions like commit_assault_element, requiring subteam_formed and readiness.  
- Coordinators can authorize through multiple actions, such as coordinator_finalize_authorization, and must designate initiators before propagating to commit members.  

## Investigating Subteam Formation  
- Subteam formation involves preparing and confirming elements, using actions like confirm_element_route_with_gear, which require gear_binding and route_reserved.  
- Preparation can also use utilities, with actions like assign_utility_to_element_route and finalize_element_route_with_utility, adjusting resource assignments.  

## Exploring Commitment Cycle  
- The process cycles through authorizing members, designating initiators via assign_initiator_to_member, and propagating to set member_committed, releasing roles and signals.  
- Propagation actions allow reusing role_tokens and signal_markers, enabling sequential commitment of all four required members.

## Assessing Coordinator Authorization  
- Coordinator authorization can use simpler paths like coordinator_confirm_target_and_authorize, requiring coordinator_target_confirmed and not authorized.  
- This involves steps like coordinator_attach_extraction_for_target, needing target_gear_ready and extract_binding, setting the target as confirmed.  

## Evaluating Target Tag Availability  
- Initial setup shows target_tag_alpha available, while target_tag_bravo is bound but not listed as available, limiting options for coordinator_bravo.  
- This suggests using different authorization paths, like target-based for one coordinator and alternate methods for the other, due to resource constraints.  

## Planning Resource Allocation  
- Limited resources like one tactic_entry, gear, utility, and signal require sharing, impacting sequential actions for member configuration and commitment.  
- Strategy involves forming subteams, provisioning elements, and authorizing coordinators first, potentially using target paths where bindings exist.

## Streamlining Member Commitment  
- The process for committing members involves sequential steps: enroll, assign role and tactic, confirm configuration, authorize, designate initiator, and propagate to commit.  
- Designating an initiator requires member_authorized_for_execution, member_tactic_binding, and an available signal, then sets initiator_designated and member_signal_binding, releasing resources upon propagation.  

## Managing Limited Resources  
- With only one tactic_entry, each member needs tactic_binding for designation, requiring remove_tactic_from_member after confirm_config to free for the next member.  
- The sequence ensures roles and signals are released after propagation, allowing reuse, but tactics remain bound until explicitly removed.  

## Authorizing Members  
- Elements require forming subteam_alpha, preparing and confirming, then committing via commit_assault_element or commit_support_element to set authorized.  
- Coordinators, assigned to the same subteam, can be authorized separately using actions like coordinator_confirm_target_and_authorize, simplifying the process.

## Optimizing Resource Use  
- Limited gear and utility restrict simultaneous confirmation for assault and support elements, requiring sequential processing.  
- Using utility path allows reuse after finalization, as finalize_element_route_with_utility sets confirmed and releases utility for the next element.  

## Exploring Authorization Paths  
- For coordinator_bravo, target-based authorization is limited by unavailable target_tag_bravo, suggesting alternative subteam-based paths.  
- Coordinator_finalize_authorization requires sync_ready, achievable through actions like coordinator_activate_extraction_marker, but involves multiple prerequisites.  

## Planning Subteam Formation  
- Forming subteam_alpha needs both assault and support elements prepared and confirmed, complicated by single gear and utility resources.  
- Consider reserving routes and sectors without utility/gear first, then using utility path for sequential confirmation to manage limited resources.

## Extending Utility Path  
- After finalizing with the utility path, route_has_utility remains true, but assemble_subteam doesn't require it to be false, focusing only on reserved and confirmed status.  
- Utility path allows reuse after finalization, setting confirmed and releasing utility, enabling sequential confirmation for assault and support elements.  

## Planning Element Authorization  
- Configure assault_alpha with tactic, then use utility path by assigning utility, finalizing with tactic_binding to set reserved and confirmed, without needing prior reservation.  
- After both elements are confirmed, assemble_subteam and commit_assault_element and commit_support_element, requiring prepared and confirmed states, to authorize elements.  

## Considering Coordinator Roles  
- Coordinators can act after subteam is ready, potentially attaching objectives using coordinator actions like attach_objective_marker_to_subteam, needing configured coordinator and objective availability.  
- Goal focuses on committing four elements, suggesting coordinator authorization via target path may be unnecessary, simplifying to element-focused steps.

## Evaluating Coordinator Authorization  
- Target path allows coordinator authorization without subteam, using configured, tactic, target, gear, and extract prerequisites, but only works for coordinator_alpha due to available target_tag_alpha.  
- For coordinator_bravo, target path fails as target_tag_bravo is bound but unavailable, with no actions to make it accessible, limiting this approach.  

## Exploring Alternative Paths for Bravo  
- Other coordinator authorization paths, like coordinator_finalize_authorization, require sync_ready, which depends on objective assigned to subteam, needing subteam_formed and ready.  
- All coordinator actions involving loadouts or extraction markers require objective assigned to subteam, suggesting subteam formation is necessary for bravo's authorization.

## Configuring Assault and Support Elements  
- Configure assault_element_alpha by assigning tactic_entry and utility_smoke, setting it to prepared and confirmed after finalizing with utility, while keeping route_has_utility true.  
- For support_element_alpha, assign role_token_b and tactic_entry, then use utility_smoke to finalize, setting it to prepared, confirmed, and sector_reserved.  

## Assembling the Subteam  
- Assemble subteam_alpha with assault_element_alpha and support_element_alpha, ensuring both are prepared, confirmed, and routes/sectors are reserved, leading to subteam_formed.  
- Tactic needs reassignment to assault_element_alpha after initial removal to meet provision_subteam prerequisites, enabling subteam_ready status.  

## Authorizing Elements and Coordinators  
- Commit authorized assault_element_alpha and support_element_alpha using subteam_alpha, requiring prepared, confirmed, formed, and ready states, with tactics still bound post-commit.  
- For coordinator_alpha, assign role_token_c and prepare for configuration, potentially reusing tactic_entry after freeing it from assault_element_alpha.

## Assessing Assemble and Confirm Steps  
- Assemble_subteam_full can occur without confirmation, requiring prepared elements, assigned routes/sectors, and utilities, setting formed and ready flags but not reserving routes/sectors.  
- Finalize_element_route_with_utility remains possible post-assemble, as it doesn’t require subteam involvement, setting route_reserved and element_confirmed while releasing utilities.  

## Finalizing Support and Assault Elements  
- Finalizing support element sets sector_reserved and confirmed, releasing utility without affecting sector_has_utility, maintaining readiness for subteam.  
- Both elements, after finalizing, meet commit requirements with confirmed status, ensuring smooth progression to authorization.  

## Preparing Coordinator Paths  
- Attach_objective_to_subteam requires subteam_ready, achieved after provision, with assemble_full setting route/sector readiness prerequisites.  
- Coordinator_configure_loadout needs sub_route_ready, which assemble_full ensures, setting coordinator_prepared and secondary_confirmed for loadout configuration.

## Preparing Coordinator Paths  
- Subteam path for bravo requires breach and gear for loadout configuration, distinct from alpha’s target path which binds gear and extract.  
- Gear and extract, once bound to alpha for target confirmation, can be detached post-commit to free for bravo’s subteam needs.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Breach is only needed for bravo’s subteam path and isn’t used by alpha, ensuring no overlap in resource use.  
- Secondary, objective markers, and sync markers are sufficiently available, with two of each, supporting bravo’s subteam configuration.  

## Sequencing Subteam Setup  
- Configure elements like assault_element_alpha by enrolling, assigning roles, tactics, and utilities, ensuring prepared states for assemble_full.  
- Assemble_full requires both elements prepared, with finalize actions possible after, releasing utilities while maintaining readiness for commit.

## Configuring Assault and Support Elements  
- Enroll assault_element_alpha, assign role_token_a, tactic_entry, and utility_smoke to entry_route_alpha, ensuring prepared and has_util status.  
- Finalize assault_element_alpha’s route, confirming and reserving it, then free tactic_entry, leaving utility available.  

## Setting Up Support Path  
- Enroll support_element_alpha, assign role_token_b, tactic_entry, and reserve cover_sector_alpha, ensuring configured status.  
- Assign gear_breacher_kit to support_element_alpha, confirm sector with gear, then free tactic_entry, setting prepared and confirmed.  

## Assembling and Provisioning Subteam  
- Assemble_subteam with both elements, forming subteam_alpha with assigned routes/sectors, meeting readiness prerequisites.  
- Provision subteam_alpha by assigning tactic_entry to assault_element_alpha, setting subteam ready, then optionally free tactic_entry.  

## Committing Elements for Authorization  
- Commit assault_element_alpha and support_element_alpha to subteam_alpha, authorizing both with roles a and b, leaving gear on support and utility free.  
- This step ensures elements are ready for coordinator target path configuration, maintaining resource availability.

## Adjusting Assembly Sequence  
- Assembling the subteam doesn't initially set route_ready or sector_ready, creating a configuration issue for loadout.  
- The assemble_subteam_with_route_sync action can set route_ready, requiring assault_prepared, support_prepared, and route_has_utility, but not assault_confirmed at assembly time.  

## Finalizing Element Routes  
- After assemble_with_route_sync, finalize_element_route_with_utility can confirm the assault element, setting it to confirmed and reserved while keeping utility assigned.  
- This approach ensures the subteam achieves route_ready, allowing further coordination steps without sector_ready initially.  

## Configuring Coordinator Actions  
- coordinator_activate_extraction_with_route_flag can proceed with route_ready, not requiring sector_ready, setting sync_ready for extraction.  
- coordinator_prepare_with_breach works without sub_sector_ready, enabling loadout_config with available breach gear, detached from coordinator_alpha post-commit.

## Managing Tactic Assignments  
- After assigning utility to the assault element, remove the tactic from assault and assign it to support, ensuring support is ready for configuration.  
- Finalize the assault element later by reassigning the tactic, confirming its route, and freeing the utility, maintaining resource availability.  

## Assembling Subteam with Routes  
- Assemble the subteam with route sync, ensuring both assault and support are prepared, with assigned routes and sectors, setting subteam_route_ready.  
- The process leaves the tactic on support initially, requiring later reassignment for finalizing the assault element.  

## Preparing Coordinator Bravo  
- Enroll coordinator bravo, assign role token c and tactic entry, then confirm configuration for secondary objective claiming.  
- Attach objective marker to the subteam, ensuring coordinator bravo is set for further target path actions.

## Synchronizing Coordination Steps  
- Attaching sync marker to coordinator bravo sets sync_ready and binding not available, ensuring preparation for execution.  
- Opening execution window requires sync_ready, attached marker, and tactic binding, confirming assault and support elements are ready.  

## Finalizing Authorization  
- Finalizing and authorizing coordinator bravo sets member_auth, with prerequisites including sync_ready and finalized status.  
- The process uses sync_marker_a, which becomes bound, ensuring all coordination steps are committed.  

## Managing Tactic and Role Bindings  
- Assigning tactics to assault and support elements ensures tactic_binding for initiator assignment, maintaining role bindings until propagation.  
- Propagating initiators to elements commits them, releasing roles and signals, with tactics freed after each propagation for reuse.  

## Verifying Precondition Satisfaction  
- Opening execution window needs coordinator-assigned elements and confirmed statuses, all satisfied in initial setup for bravo.  
- Assembling with route sync relies on sector_reserved from support gear path, ensuring all preconditions are met for coordination.

## Configuring Coordinator Alpha  
- Coordinator alpha is assigned gear breacher_kit and extraction marker 1, preparing for target authorization.  
- After confirming and authorizing, coordinator alpha commits with signal_marker_primary, then frees gear, extraction, and role_c.  

## Setting Up Coordinator Bravo  
- Enroll coordinator bravo, assign role_token_c, and configure for secondary objective_b, attaching objective marker to subteam_alpha.  
- Prepare coordinator bravo with breach charge and gear, then activate extraction with route flag, setting sync_ready.  

## Finalizing Coordination  
- Open execution window for coordinator bravo, confirming assault and support elements are ready, with sync_ready and tactic_binding met.  
- Finalize and authorize coordinator bravo, commit with signal_marker_primary, then free tactic, signal, and role_c.  

## Committing Assault and Support Elements  
- Assign tactic and initiator to assault_element_alpha, propagate to commit, then free the tactic for reuse.  
- Assign tactic and initiator to support_element_alpha, propagate to commit, continuing the coordination process.

- Attach objective marker to the subteam, ensuring coordinator bravo is set for further target path actions.

## Synchronizing Coordination Steps  
- Attaching sync marker to coordinator bravo sets sync_ready and binding not available, ensuring preparation for execution.  
- Opening execution window requires sync_ready, attached marker, and tactic binding, confirming assault and support elements are ready.  

## Finalizing Authorization  
- Finalizing and authorizing coordinator bravo sets member_auth, with prerequisites including sync_ready and finalized status.  
- The process uses sync_marker_a, which becomes bound, ensuring all coordination steps are committed.  

## Managing Tactic and Role Bindings  
- Assigning tactics to assault and support elements ensures tactic_binding for initiator assignment, maintaining role bindings until propagation.  
- Propagating initiators to elements commits them, releasing roles and signals, with tactics freed after each propagation for reuse.  

## Verifying Precondition Satisfaction  
- Opening execution window needs coordinator-assigned elements and confirmed statuses, all satisfied in initial setup for bravo.  
- Assembling with route sync relies on sector_reserved from support gear path, ensuring all preconditions are met for coordination.

## Configuring Coordinator Alpha  
- Coordinator alpha is assigned gear breacher_kit and extraction marker 1, preparing for target authorization.  
- After confirming and authorizing, coordinator alpha commits with signal_marker_primary, then frees gear, extraction, and role_c.  

## Setting Up Coordinator Bravo  
- Enroll coordinator bravo, assign role_token_c, and configure for secondary objective_b, attaching objective marker to subteam_alpha.  
- Prepare coordinator bravo with breach charge and gear, then activate extraction with route flag, setting sync_ready.  

## Finalizing Coordination  
- Open execution window for coordinator bravo, confirming assault and support elements are ready, with sync_ready and tactic_binding met.  
- Finalize and authorize coordinator bravo, commit with signal_marker_primary, then free tactic, signal, and role_c.  

## Committing Assault and Support Elements  
- Assign tactic and initiator to assault_element_alpha, propagate to commit, then free the tactic for reuse.  
- Assign tactic and initiator to support_element_alpha, propagate to commit, continuing the coordination process.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
